ALL SPECIALTIES / PHARMACY TECHNICIAN / MONTANA

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Montana Pharmacy Technician License

Montana Pharmacy Technician license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-03-18 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Pharmacy Technician applications in Montana run through Montana Board of Pharmacy. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official Transcripts - Sent directly from your educational institution.
  • Exam Score Report - Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 2 to 4 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Montana by submitting a new application to the Montana Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Montana-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 2 to 4 Weeks.

  1. STEP 1

    Review Eligibility Requirements

    Ensure you meet the Montana Board of Pharmacy's requirements, including being at least 18 years old, having good moral character, and having either completed a Board-approved training program OR holding current PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or NHA (National Healthcareer Association) certification.

    Fee: $35 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Create an Account & Apply Online

    Visit the eLicense Montana (Professional Licensing Online Portal) at `https://ebiz.mt.gov/pol`. Register for a new user account if you don't have one. Log in, select 'Apply for a new license', choose 'Pharmacy Technician' as the license type, and complete all sections of the online application form.

    Fee: $35 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Pay Application Fee

    Submit the non-refundable application fee of $50.00 through the online portal using a credit or debit card during the application submission process.

    Fee: $35 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Submit Supporting Documentation

    Upload required documents directly to your online application or arrange for their submission. This includes proof of age (e.g., driver's license or birth certificate) and official transcripts or a certificate of completion from a Board-approved training program, OR proof of current PTCB/NHA certification.

    Fee: $35 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Complete Criminal Background Check

    Follow the specific instructions provided within the online application or by the Board to complete a fingerprint-based criminal background check. This typically involves registering with and submitting fingerprints through a Board-approved vendor like IdentoGO.

    Fee: $35 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    Await Board Review and Licensure

    Allow time for the Montana Board of Pharmacy to thoroughly review your complete application, all submitted documentation, and the results of your criminal background check. Once approved, your Pharmacy Technician license will be issued.

    Fee: $35 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$35
Background check
See board if applicable
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$35

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Renewal

Pharmacy Technician renewal in Montana follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Annual renewal window May 2 to June 30
Renewal fee
$30
Continuing education
See board

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a Montana pharmacy technician license cost?

The Montana Board of Pharmacy lists a $35 application fee for a certified pharmacy technician. The active renewal fee is $30.

Is national certification required?

Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

Does Montana have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Montana. To work in Montana, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Montana?

The Montana Board of Pharmacy does not publish a public pharmacy technician processing timeline on its licensing page.

Can I start working in Montana while my endorsement application is processing?

Contact the Montana Board of Pharmacy to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in Montana.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$35

Processing time

2 to 4 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

See board

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Annual renewal window May 2 to June 30 · $30

Licensing board

Montana Board of Pharmacy
